channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Complete Mixtape Trophy List—How To Get All Achievements & Trophies
item.content:encoded:
In Mixtape, players experience the last day of school for a trio of teens as they navigate the chaos of growing up amid uncertainties about the future. Between the drama, players can earn various trophies and achievements in incredibly fun minigames that reinforce the fantastical nature of Beethoven & Dinosaur’s new game.

If you are aiming for Mixtape‘s Platinum Trophy, you will need to ace the minigames, exhaust all dialogue options between the characters, and interact with every single object inside each of their bedrooms.

Here’s how many trophies and achievements are available for Mixtape, as well as how to earn each one.

How Many Achievements & Trophies In Mixtape?

There are 27 Mixtape trophies to earn on PlayStation, with 26 achievements across Xbox and Steam, thanks to the lack of the ever-coveted Platinum.

  • More Insider Gaming: Mixtape Review

Thankfully for trophy hunters, the Mixtape Platinum isn’t a grind and can be earned in just a few hours, allowing you to quickly add that coveted 100% completion trophy to your account.

All Mixtape Trophies & Achievements

Most of the trophies require you to perform specific actions during the minigame segments. There is also a large number of unmissable trophies that will pop automatically just by progressing through the story.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Make Slater’s Secret Slushy in Mixtape

Here’s every trophy and achievement in Mixtape.

Will you be trying to earn every trophy in Mixtape? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more trophy lists, check out the following lists:

  • Saros
  • Forza Horizon 6
  • Pragmata

Written by

Editor (Brazil)

Ruan está cobrindo a indústria dos games desde 2017, trazendo várias informações sob a ótica do mercado e reviews após o 100%. Quando ele não está escrevendo, está jogando algum…

More from Ruan Almeida

MOBILE APP

item.title: ESL DreamLeague Season 29 Groups Announced with EWC Qualification on the Line
item.content:encoded:
ESL’s Dota 2 division has released the groups for ESL DreamLeague Season 29. The 12-day event is not only a $1,000,000 competition; it is effectively acting as one of the highest-stakes qualifiers in Dota 2’s calendar.

DreamLeague Season 29 Groups Announced

The DreamLeague Season 29 groups have set up some scintillating contests as the teams prepare for the Round-Robin Bo2 contests, with Falcons, Spirit, Aurora, and Liquid all situated in Group A. The event also sees Ex-Heroic play their first tournament since Heroic’s withdrawal from the Dota 2 scene.

For Group B, the top end sees PGL Wallachia Season 8 winners BetBoom situated alongside ESL One Birmingham Champions, Tundra. Xtreme and Parivision complete the group’s top end, with NAVI, Nigma, and Rekonix needing strong results at the event.

The groups for ESL DreamLeague Season 29 are:

Group A:

  • Virtus.pro
  • Spirit
  • GamerLegion
  • Falcons
  • Liquid
  • Ex-Heroic
  • Vici Gaming
  • Aurora

Group B:

  • Parivision
  • Xtreme Gaming
  • Tundra
  • BetBoom
  • Playtime
  • NAVI
  • Rekonix
  • Nigma Galaxy

Esports World Cup qualification to be decided at DreamLeague Season 29

DreamLeague Season 29 is the final event of the ESL Pro Tour that will contribute points towards qualification for the $2,000,000 Esports World Cup 2026 Dota 2 tournament.

So far, seven teams have qualified for the Esports World Cup 2026 Dota 2 tournament, with five teams assuring their spot through unassailable positions in the EPT Leaderboard, in addition to Spirit, who are invited as defending champions, and Vici Gaming, who qualified via their ESL Challenger China Season 3 victory.

So far, the teams that have qualified for the Esports World Cup 2026 Dota 2 tournament are:

  • Tundra Esports – 14,510 EPT Points
  • Team Yandex – 10,400 EPT Points
  • Xtreme Gaming – 9560 EPT Points
  • Aurora Gaming – 8230 EPT Points
  • Parivision – 8210 EPT Points
  • Team Spirit – Defending Champions
  • Vici Gaming – ESL Challenger China Season 3 Champions

That leaves seven spaces left to be decided by EPT Points, with a further 10 positions coming from the June qualifiers to make up the remainder of the 24 spots at the event.

MOUZ and OG are currently sitting within the EPT Leaderboard spots for EWC, but are crucially missing the event after defeats in the Western European and Southeast Asian qualifiers, respectively.

That opens up opportunities for teams like the former Heroic roster, Rekonix, NAVI, South America Rejects, and Nigma Galaxy to sneak into those spots.

Teams that are currently situated within invite range, and are attending DreamLeague Season 29, include Falcons, Liquid, BetBoom, Virtus.pro, and GamerLegion, all of whom will hope to assure their places at EWC with decent results at the event.

Who do you think will qualify for EWC 2026? Let us know on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For more news, see the full Visa issues for the Salt Lake City Rainbow 6 Major.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Neverness to Everness Developer Responds to AI Concerns
item.content:encoded:
The developers behind Gacha Hit Neverness to Everness have responded to concerns over AI usage, admitting that some tools were created on a “small number” of minor details in the game.

Hotta Studio’s title has attracted global attention following its launch, with the title highly anticipated and garnering over 90 million pre-registrations, but players on social media began to express concern over alleged AI use.

Responding through the international account for the game, Hotta Studio admitted some AI tools were used, but explained that they are “reviewing and reworking” flagged assets.

Neverness to Everness AI Concern Addressed

In a post on X through the NTE_Global account, Hotta Studio stated that “Neverness to Everness is built on human creativity,” and that “the characters, stories, and world you experience are the work of artists, writers, and designers.”

However, there was an admission of the use of some AI-assisted tools, saying that they “were only used on a small number of background and environmental assets, not on the characters or stories that define this game.”

Assets for “Clear Skies in Summer” and “Pink Paws Heist” are already being reviewed and reworked, with Hotta Studio encouraging players to keep feedback coming, assuring players they are listening and “committed to delivering the quality you deserve.”

AI use in video games and in other areas across the globe has become a particular sticking point for many, with growing concerns over job losses, environmental impact, plagiarism, and other issues.

Hotta Studio has been caught up in the spin of backlash toward AI use, and however minor, there will always be loud concerns about the tools in whichever capacity they are used.

What do you think about the AI use in Neverness to Everness? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, check out details of the Halo Next campaign project surfacing, and take a look at leaked gameplay from an announced Worms game.

item.title: ‘Halo Next’ Campaign Project Details Surface Online in New Report and Job Listing
item.content:encoded:
A new report from a Halo insider discusses the developers’ mainline campaign project, Halo Next.

As players wait for Halo Campaign Evolved to release this year, we have to do with reports on what’s on the plate for the franchise. On the topic of Remakes, a report earlier this week stated that development of the Halo 2 and 3 remakes was underway. Now, a report discusses what’s next for the franchise’s upcoming installments.

Halo Next Could Focus on The Endless, New Job Listing Revealed

A new YouTube video from Rebs Gaming discusses Halo Next’s details and how his information connects to a new job listing. Rebs’ information says Joe Staten told 343 Industries he doesn’t intend to see the Flood return, at least not in the Halo Infinite Campaign sequel. Staten doesn’t have a problem with The Flood, but at the moment, he doesn’t have a return for them on the cards right now.

The report states that the Halo Next campaign project would focus on adding new elements to the universe and featuring The Endless. The video also mentions that the sequel to Halo Infinite was referred to as ‘Campaign Next’ several times during Joe Staten’s studio-wide Microsoft Teams meeting. This sequel has been in development since 2022.

The job listing in question is for a Narrative Design Director who will be tasked to “help define and deliver the narrative experience for Halo Next.” The overview section confirms it’s a campaign, stating that the Narrative Design Director must “ensure narrative and gameplay work together to deliver a cohesive and memorable campaign experience.” The responsibilities of this role require him to “ensure the campaign delivers a complete and emotionally satisfying story that is accessible to new players and meaningful to longtime fans.” They will also need to make sure  “Halo Next aligns with long-term franchise direction and canon.”

The report talks about Halo Studios bringing players the next mainline campaign installment, which is internally being called Halo Next. The question remains whether new leadership will honor Joe Staten’s wishes or pursue a different route, such as the Edge of Dawn novel. Readers should take some of this information with a grain of salt.

In other news, Halo Campaign Evolved’s early access and release dates have seemingly surfaced in a new leak. Here are more details. Additionally, an insider has claimed that Halo Campaign Evolved will feature a customized skin system and is playable from start to finish. What are your thoughts on Halo Next being the next mainline campaign project from Halo Studios? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Worms gameplay leaking from an unannounced game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: First Worms Gameplay Has Leaked From Unannounced Game
item.content:encoded:
On May 6, a new Worms game was suddenly leaked with full screenshots. A gameplay leak was also teased to tantalise long-time Worms fans and potential newcomers, and now this is a reality.

If you’ve been following the news, you might have heard that a new Worms game has possibly leaked in a bizarre way. Well, that leak has gone one step further with several minutes of in-game footage from (what appears to be) an ongoing playtest.

The same account that randomly leaked the new Worms game out of nowhere has struck again, as promised.

Evil Leeker Man—now going by @EvilLeeker—posted the footage to Streamable, and as the reports suggested, it’s very much a ‘tactics’ game in the same vein as XCOM or the Mario & Rabbids series.

Worms’ movements are limited to certain spaces and actions. You need to strategically use cover to obscure your position, and the maps seem to have interactive objects for more damage, such as exploding barrels.

There are also health and weapon pick-ups, and the worms still quip in that cheeky way the franchise became known for. It’s a new style of gameplay for a Worms game, and it could be the refresh the series needed.

It might be worth keeping an eye on this new leaker account as more Worms details and footage could be imminent.

You can check out the full video in the attached link.

How does the new Worms game look to you then? Was it time for something new like this? Outside of perhaps a Worms volleyball game, what could the series do to innovate? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out Star Fox being announced for Nintendo Switch 2, and the NFL 2K game no longer being in development, for more hot news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: CS Asia Championships 2026 Unveils Groups: s1mple and NiKo Set for Opening Clash
item.content:encoded:
Perfect World has unveiled the groups for the CS Asia Championships, alongside the opening matches for the event, which begins on May 20.

Key storylines for the CS Asia Championships 2026

The CS Asia Championships 2025 was officially a Tier 2 event, with only FaZe’s Wildcard invite breaking up the Tier 2 invites.

This time around, the event is a fully fledged Tier 1 event, with Falcons, The MongolZ, Parivision, and Mouz amongst the highest-ranked teams attending the event. Other big names attending include Liquid, Ninjas in Pyjamas, 3DMAX, and 2025 CAC Champions, Legacy.

Therefore, the $1,000,000 is set to be a more competitive affair, notably acting as the last Tier 1 event before the IEM Cologne 2026 Major.

That is especially pertinent for teams like Falcons and MOUZ, who are still grappling with recent roster changes. MOUZ and Falcons are set to make their first showings since changes were announced at PGL Astana 2026.

For Falcons, it will be their second event with Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen, as they prepare an all-in play for the IEM Cologne Major. They have only a short time to get up to speed with the Danish IGL, so there will be a lot to look out for in terms of their teamwork and cohesion.

For MOUZ, the event will be their last with Justinas ‘jL’ Lekavicius, as they experiment with Dorian ‘xertioN’ Berman as IGL before Ludvig ‘Brollan’ Brolin returns for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major due to roster restrictions at the event.

With that in mind, the event could have huge implications for what MOUZ feels they need in the offseason as they prepare to fill the spaces in the roster left by Brollan and Jimi ‘Jimpphat’ Salo’s benchings.

Most pressingly, their minds will likely be made up by the end of Shanghai about whether jL could be a solution to their problems, and whether xertioN is suitable for long-term IGLing.

CS Asia Championships 2026 groups and opening match-ups

The CS Asia Championship features two groups of eight teams in a double-elimination format, with six teams advancing to the playoffs. There will also be a Third/fourth-place decider.

The opening matchups see the protagonists of the PGL Stockholm 2021 Major Grand Final clash in the opening match, with Niko ‘NiKo’ Kovác and Oleksandr ‘s1mple’ Kostyliev duking it out as Falcons and BC.Game face each other.

Elsewhere, Liquid have a tough test against Parivision in Group B, with the roster still struggling to find its footing even after the addition of Mario ‘malbsMd’ Samayo.

The groups and opening matches for the CS Asia Championships 2026 are:

Group A:

  • Legacy vs NRG
  • MOUZ vs Tyloo
  • Falcons vs BC.Game
  • paiN vs M80

Group B:

  • 3DMAX vs MIBR
  • Parivision vs Liquid
  • The MongolZ vs Lynn Vision
  • B8 vs Ninjas in Pyjamas

You can discuss the CS Asia Championships on our Discord!
For more CS, see our IEM Atlanta 2026 Tournament Guide.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
